obligation_text: The measures for human oversight shall be commensurate with the risks, level of autonomy and context of use of the high-risk AI system, and shall ensure that the natural persons to whom human oversight is assigned: are able to properly understand the relevant capacities and limitations of the high-risk AI system; are able to duly monitor its operation; remain aware of the possible tendency of automatically relying on the output produced by a high-risk AI system; are able to correctly interpret the high-risk AI system's output; and are able to decide not to use the high-risk AI system, to disregard, override or reverse its output.
scoring_text: Ability to exercise human oversight of AI systems, including understanding system capacities and limitations, monitoring system operation, recognizing automation bias, correctly interpreting AI outputs, and exercising authority to override or reverse AI system decisions.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Ability to exercise human oversight of AI systems, including understanding system capacities and limitations, monitoring system operation, recognizing automation bias, correctly interpreting AI outputs, and exercising authority to override or reverse AI system decisions.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in designing human oversight mechanisms for AI-assisted decision-making, including escalation triggers, override procedures, human-in-the-loop protocols, and meaningful human control verification.
comments: EU AI Act Art. 14(4) (competency) equal WIDAI AG domain. AG-S-007 is the strongest match (STS: 0.698, cosine: 0.729). Secondary: AG-K-004 (AG, cosine: 0.715), DG-A-003 (DG, cosine: 0.711).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the regulatory requirement) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 7.0/10 (raw 0.698). Shared vocabulary: [ai, human, override, oversight]. EU AI Act competency requirement terms [ability, authority, automation, bias, capacities, correctly] vs WIDAI terms [assisted, control, decision, designing, escalation, loop] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.885 (P=0.878, R=0.893). Balanced P/R — the 34-word EU AI Act competency and 21-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs (mean ~0.007) because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (97.8%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the EU AI Act element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.729 ≈ STS 0.698 (gap 0.032). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [ai, human, override, oversight] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 13.3%. Shared tokens: [ai, human, override, oversight]. EU AI Act-only: [ability, authority, automation, bias, capacities, correctly]. WIDAI-only: [assisted, control, decision, designing, escalation, loop].
